title: Pre-defined macros should not be defined, redefined or undefined
description: Pre-defined macros should not be defined, redefined or undefinedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misrapreprocessorsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe standard, predefined macros, such as__FILE__and__LINE__, are primarily intended for use by the implementation, and changing them could result in undefined behavior.This rule checks that the following predefined macros are not defined, undefined, or redefined:assert,errno,__FILE__,__LINE__,__TIME__,__DATE__,__TIMESTAMP__,__COUNTER__,__INCLUDE_LEVEL__,__BASE_FILE__, and_Pragma.Noncompliant code example#undef __LINE__Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper Ed
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Redefining predefined macros
#undef __FILE__        // Noncompliant
#define __FILE__ "my_file.c"

#undef __LINE__        // Noncompliant
#define __LINE__ 100

#define errno 0        // Noncompliant

#undef assert          // Noncompliant

// Defining predefined macros
#define __DATE__ "2024-01-01"    // Noncompliant
#define __TIME__ "12:00:00"      // Noncompliant
#define _Pragma(x) /* nothing */ // Noncompliant

checks.message: Predefined macro violation - do not define, redefine, or undefine standard macros
